stories.headline: Oil back above $100 as US to blockade Iranian ports after peace talks fail
stories.summary: The failure of negotiations at the weekend has raised concerns that the global energy crisis will deepen.

stories.headline: Iran war live: US military says it will block Iranian traffic in Hormuz
stories.summary: CENTCOM says the blockade will begin at 14:00 GMT, as Iran warns of even higher prices at the pump.

stories.headline: What We Know About the Eric Swalwell Sexual Misconduct Allegations
stories.summary: Accusations that Mr. Swalwell, a congressman who was running for governor in California, sexually assaulted a former staffer and behaved inappropriately with other women have upended the race.

stories.headline: Oil jumps and Japan stocks slip as Trump threatens Hormuz blockade
Congressman Eric Swalwell quits California governor race amid sexual misconduct claims
stories.summary: Top Democratic allies intensified pressure on him to exit the gubernatorial race.

stories.headline: SVB Energy's Vakhshouri on US Blockade of Hormuz
stories.summary: Sara Vakhshouri, Founder and President at SVB Energy International, discusses her outlook for energy markets as the US moved to blockade the Strait of Hormuz after weekend talks between Washington and Tehran failed to reach a deal, escalating a global energy crisis that’s rocked markets. She speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Shery Ahn on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: What Would a US Blockade of Hormuz Mean for Energy Markets
stories.summary: As President Trump threatens a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski reports on the possible impact on oil and fuel supplies.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Ping An Seeks to Sell $1 Billion Software-Focused PE Assets
stories.summary: China’s biggest insurer Ping An Insurance Group is seeking to reduce software-focused private equity exposure by selling its stakes in some funds, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Trump Says Gas Prices Might Not Drop By Midterms, Highlighting G.O.P. Peril
stories.summary: The president has sent mixed messages on how long he expects costs to be high. Some in his party voiced fresh concern after his comments on Sunday.

stories.headline: Former Staffers Call on Eric Swalwell to Quit Campaign and Resign from House
stories.summary: In a signed letter, they called on law enforcement officials to “open a full and thorough investigation” of the California representative.

stories.summary: Prime minister says he wants negotiations between US and Iran to resume amid ‘massive global economic impact’. stories.headline: Japan's long-term government bond yield hit a 29-year high

Half of Australians Near Retirement Fear Running Out of Cash
stories.summary: Almost half of Australians approaching retirement fear running out of money and lack confidence managing their finances once they stop working, according to new research from the corporate regulator.

stories.headline: BOJ’s Usual Stance Amid Uncertainty Is to Hold, Ex-Official Says
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan’s usual approach to conducting policy at times of high uncertainty would be to wait and see, making the likely outcome of a meeting this month too close to call, according to a former executive director who oversaw monetary policy at the central bank.

Japan's SoftBank launches unit to develop homegrown AI
To Lam visits China, Indian economic data, Guangzhou trade fair
Japanese board game company makes its pitch to the global market
Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Oil prices soar above $100 per barrel as hope fades of end to Iran war
stories.summary: Crude had stabilised on optimism that a deal could be reached to reopen the Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz as Iran peace talks fail
stories.summary: Reopening the waterway is a sticking point in negotiations as oil supply bottlenecks fuel a global energy crisis

stories.headline: Trump Was Watching a U.F.C. Fight in Miami While Iran Talks Collapsed
stories.summary: On his way to Florida, President Trump said it did not matter to him if a deal with Iran was reached or not: “We win, regardless,” he said.

stories.headline: Oil Surges With Gas as US Blockade of Hormuz Escalates Crisis
stories.summary: Oil and natural gas surged as the US moved to blockade the Strait of Hormuz after weekend talks between Washington and Tehran failed to reach a deal, escalating a global energy crisis that’s rocked markets.

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal
stories.summary: The blockade could derail a tenuous ceasefire after just five days. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes “our final and best offer.”

stories.headline: Penny Wong calls failed peace talks between US and Iran ‘disappointing’ and urges resumption

stories.headline: Trump orders Strait of Hormuz blockade after US-Iran talks fail in Islamabad
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump ordered a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after talks with Iran in Islamabad ended without a deal. The move ramps up pressure as both sides blame each other, while Pakistan urges restraint and the ceasefire hangs in the balance.

stories.headline: European Gas Prices Jump as Trump Threatens Hormuz Blockade
stories.summary: European natural gas prices surged in early Asia trading on Monday, after President Donald Trump said the US will begin a full blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Hungary opposition delivers ‘regime change’ after 16 years of Orbán
stories.summary: With its huge mandate Péter Magyar’s Tisza can prepare to change the constitution and restore rule of law

stories.headline: US stock futures slide ahead of Iran ports blockade
stories.summary: Oil prices expected to rise as Trump tells US navy to ‘interdict’ ships that pay Iran to pass through strategic chokepoint

stories.headline: Oil Jumps, Stocks Drop on US Plan to Block Hormuz: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Oil surged while stocks and bonds fell as a brief bout of market optimism unraveled after President Donald Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions with Iran after weekend peace talks collapsed.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

stories.headline: Peter Magyar wins Hungary election, unseating Viktor Orban after 16 years
stories.summary: Centre-right Tisza party wins two-thirds majority in parliament that would allow it to amend constitution.

stories.headline: Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power
stories.summary: Péter Magyar’s Tisza party wins election as prime minister concedes defeat, in result likely to reshape ties with EUHungary election liveHungary’s opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, has won the election, bringing an end to Viktor Orbán’s 16-year grip on power, in a result that is likely to rattle the White House and reshape the country’s relationship with the EU.Less than three hours after polls closed on Sunday, Orbán conceded defeat a

stories.headline: Hungary election: Supporters of Peter Magyar's party celebrate in Budapest
stories.summary: Supporters of Peter Magyar, leader of the pro-European conservative TISZA party, celebrate on the banks on the river Danube in Budapest.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching 36-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: According to Ukraine's General Staff of the armed forces, by 7am the ceasefire had been violated by Russia 2,299 times, including assaults, shelling and small drone launches. However, Russia has refrained from using long-range drones, missiles or guided bombs.

stories.headline: Civilians feared killed after reports of air strike on Nigerian market
stories.summary: The Nigerian Air Force has not addressed reports it struck a village market while targeting militants.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Orbán concedes defeat as opposition secures landslide win
stories.summary: Tisza party led by Péter Magyar projected to win a two-thirds majority in crushing loss for EU’s longest-serving premier

stories.headline: Britain Faces Another Lost Year for Living Standards Due to Iran War
stories.summary: A typical UK household will be left nearly £500 ($672) worse off by surging energy prices, a setback for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s pledge to boost living standards.

stories.headline: Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files
stories.summary: Many who support the president have expressed frustration with how Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi handled the Epstein files.

stories.headline: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.headline: China’s Victory Giant Seeks Up to $2.2 Billion in Hong Kong Listing
stories.summary: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co. began taking investor orders for a listing in Hong Kong that may raise as much as HK$17.5 billion ($2.2 billion) in what is set to be among the city’s largest first-time share sales this year.

stories.headline: Aussie’s One-Year Rally Versus Kiwi Is Peaking, Strategists Say
stories.summary: The Australian dollar’s year-long rally against its New Zealand counterpart may have topped out, as strategists signal hawkish central bank rhetoric in Wellington is bolstering the kiwi’s appeal.

stories.headline: CapitaLand Raises $320 Million for Asia-Pacific Credit Fund
stories.summary: Singapore’s CapitaLand Investment Ltd. raised $320 million for its Asia-Pacific real estate credit fund, completing a fundraise for the firm controlled by Temasek Holdings Pte.

stories.headline: Trump Fires Board That Oversees Presidio in San Francisco
stories.summary: All six board members that oversee the national historic landmark were terminated, part of the administration’s efforts to reshape the federal bureaucracy.

stories.headline: What the ‘Real Housewives’ Think About Congress’s Reality TV Drama
stories.summary: A visit to the Capitol by stars of the “Real Housewives” offered a glimpse into how the nation’s elected representatives mimic the gossipy world of reality TV. Nobody appears to relish the similarities.

stories.headline: Push to Expel Swalwell Could Touch Off Chain Reaction of House Removal Votes
stories.summary: The House could move within days to consider expelling Representative Eric Swalwell, Democrat of California, in light of sexual assault allegations against him. There could be more to come for both parties.

stories.headline: Heritage president toasted editor of controversial right-wing magazine
stories.summary: Heritage Foundation chief Kevin Roberts called Paul Gottfried, a “paleoconservative” who promotes writers accused of white supremacism, “one of the sages of our age.”

stories.headline: Polls close in Benin with finance minister expected to win election
stories.summary: Former President Patrice Talon has stepped down after two five-year terms.

stories.headline: Peru votes for ninth president in less than decade
stories.summary: Voters to choose from 35 presidential candidates, including a comedian, a media baron and a political dynasty heiress.

stories.headline: EU’s largest economies vs top US states: How do they compare in GDP?
stories.summary: Top EU countries and US states appear one after the other in economic size rankings. However, US states surpass the top EU economies in GDP per capita, both in nominal terms and purchasing power.

stories.headline: Vance, who wasn’t keen on Iran war, now tasked with trying to end it
stories.summary: The weekend’s peace talks in Islamabad mark Vance’s highest-profile assignment as vice president, with concomitant political risk.

stories.headline: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
stories.summary: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".

stories.headline: EU airline industry warns of fuel shortages if Strait of Hormuz stays closed
stories.summary: Airports Council International (ACI) Europe said its members had "increasing concerns" about the availability of jet fuel.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell, California governor candidate, accused of sexual assault by ex-staffer
stories.summary: Supporters of Rep. Eric Swalwell’s campaign withdrew support, including two congressmen who chaired his campaign and two senators who endorsed his bid. Swalwell denied the allegations.

stories.headline: The high-stakes diplomacy that led to Pakistan hosting US-Iran peace talks
stories.summary: Critical minerals, cryptocurrency, and CIA co-operation - how Asim Munir, Trump’s “favourite field marshal”, may have pulled off the diplomatic coup of the century.

stories.headline: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
stories.summary: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.summary: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.headline: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patronThe Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded.The charity has lodged papers in London’s high court over defamation claims naming Prince Harry and the former Sentebale trustee Mark Dyer as defendants. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Russia and Ukraine agree to Orthodox Easter truce
stories.summary: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon through Easter Sunday.

stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes in Lebanon 'grave violation' of ceasefire, Iran minister tells BBC
stories.summary: Iran's deputy foreign minister says the US must choose between 'war and ceasefire'.

stories.headline: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
stories.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.headline: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

stories.headline: French far-right leader romantically linked to Italian princess
stories.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.headline: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.headline: Why Real Madrid tie could make or break Kane's Ballon d'Or hopes
stories.summary: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?

stories.headline: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

stories.headline: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
stories.summary: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.

stories.headline: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound
stories.summary: Almost nothing was known about the Royal Hill casino until the Thai military took control of it in December.
